title: B.Sc. (Hons) in Statistics & Data Science
content: This is a three year degree program thoughtfully designed for curious minds who enjoy solving real-world problemsthrough numbers, logic, and technology. It offers students a strong grounding in statistical theory while also opening doors to the exciting world of modern data science. From understanding the fundamentals of probability and inference to diving into machine learning, big data, and artificial intelligence, this program ensures a well-rounded academic journey. What sets it apart is its hands-on approach—students learn to code in Python and R, explore data using industry-relevant tools, and apply their skills to meaningful projects and internships. The program is flexible, with a wide variety of electives ranging from Natural Language Processing and Cloud Computing to Healthcare Analytics and Quantum Computing, allowing students to tailor their learning to their interests and career goals. Whether you see yourself building AI systems, analyzing trends in finance, solving healthcare challenges, or pursuing cutting-edge research, this program gives you the tools, the freedom, and the confidence to get there. 10+2 with Science, including Mathematics/Statistics as one of the subjects, with a minimum aggregate of 50% marks. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057) score will be given preference in the merit list. 01 This program equips students to master statistical analysis and data science tools like R and Python, building skills to design machine learning pipelines, handle ethical data practices per GDPR, and deliver actionable insights through visualizations and reports for industry-ready decision-making. 01 02 03 Modern tool usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern scientific and IT tools including prediction and modelling to complex scientific activities with an understanding of the limitations. 04 05 06 07 08 01 Graduates will excel as data analysts or scientists in industry, applying statistical modeling and machine learning to solve real-world problems in sectors like finance, healthcare, and e-commerce. 02 Graduates will lead data-driven decision-making teams, demonstrating proficiency in big data tools (e.g., Python, R, SQL) and ethical data practices. 03 Graduates will pursue advanced certifications or roles in AI/ML, contributing to innovative analytics projects with a 80%+ success rate in predictive modeling. 04 Graduates will engage in lifelong learning, adapting to emerging data technologies and collaborating on interdisciplinary projects globally. 01 Statistical and Mathematical Knowledge: Demonstrate in-depth knowledge of statistical theories, data analysis techniques, and computational tools essential for extracting meaningful insights from data, and apply this knowledge effectively across diverse real-world domains. 02 Data Science and Computational Skills: Apply the knowledge of data science tools and programming languages (e.g., R, Python, SQL) to manage, analyse, and visualize large datasets using techniques such as machine learning, data mining, and statistical modeling. 03 Real-World Applications: Formulate real-world problems into statistical and data-driven models, and design appropriate analytical approaches, algorithms, or computational solutions to interpret data and support informed decision-making. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057) score will be given preference in the merit list.
